expectations.text: Quality is framed as a measured rate across a case set — explicitly rejects the single-trial anecdote
expectations.evidence: README.md opens: '"It seems to work when I try it" is the classic trap... it works on the easy cases you happened to try'; launch bar uses rates (100% safety, >=95% format, >=90% quality) and warns 'Never judge a prompt edit by eyeballing one email'.

expectations.text: A case set of roughly 20 cases exists with a deliberate mix of typical, hard, and hostile cases
expectations.evidence: eval/test_emails.jsonl parsed: 20 cases with labeled categories spanning typical (baseline refund, feedback, cancellation), hard (multi_issue, long_input, non_english, sarcasm, precision), and hostile/edge (adversarial injection, 3x degenerate incl. empty and wrong-recipient, pii).

expectations.text: Pass criteria are defined per case BEFORE outputs are generated/graded
expectations.evidence: Every JSONL case carries key_facts (facts a good summary must capture) and traps (case-specific failure notes) which the judge grades against, plus rubric.md hard pass/fail requirements; no outputs were generated (API run left to the user), so criteria precede grading.

expectations.text: Hostile/adversarial cases include at least prompt injection and empty/misdirected input
expectations.evidence: Cases 07_prompt_injection (adversarial), 09_empty (degenerate), and 10_wrong_recipient (degenerate/misdirected) all present in eval/test_emails.jsonl.

expectations.text: Regression discipline prescribed: rerun the FULL set after any prompt or model change
expectations.evidence: README Step 6: 'Freeze this test set as your regression suite: any future prompt or model change must re-run it and match or beat the previous scores'; Step 5: 're-run the whole eval' after prompt fixes; re-run on model version changes.

expectations.text: If an LLM judge is used: it has a written rubric, blind grading, and a human spot-check/calibration requirement
expectations.evidence: eval/rubric.md is written and fed to the judge; JUDGE_TEMPLATE in run_eval.py gives the judge only rubric/email/key_facts/traps/output — never the prompt under test (structurally blind, though blindness is not stated as a design principle); README Step 4 'Calibrate the judge with human eyes (do not skip)' requires hand-grading 10 cases.
